public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the eGTM PWM for a 3-phase inverter and TMADC triggering. Steps: 1) Initialize optional watchdog configuration (if used). 2) Configure a GPIO as an LED output and drive it low. 3) Initialize the unified PWM configuration structure with defaults; select the ATOM submodule, center-aligned mode, and target switching frequency. 4) Configure one base PWM channel and subsequent synchronous channels as a contiguous group sized for three complementary phase pairs; attach complementary outputs with a dead-time element managed by the DTM set to the required dead-time duration. 5) Assign physical output pins to each configured PWM output using the generic eGTM pin mapping API. 6) Initialize the PWM driver with the prepared config and channel array so one base channel controls the timing of all synchronous channels. 7) Configure a dedicated PWM channel to generate a period-synchronous trigger pulse and connect it via the eGTM trigger crossbar to the ADC trigger input so that conversions are aligned to the PWM period. 8) Initialize the TMADC module and a set of ADC channels; REQUIRED: document the exact 5 TMADC channel IDs and their corresponding port pins (provide concrete channel identifiers and pin names) prior to implementation; configure them to be externally triggered by the routed eGTM trigger; enable the TMADC module to start processing triggers. 9) Store initial duty setpoints for the three phases and keep outputs disabled until the start function performs a synchronous start.

Apply the precomputed initial duty cycle values for all three complementary phase pairs and start PWM outputs synchronously. The function first stages the duty cycle array for the configured channels so that all updates are consistent, then performs a single synchronous start so every channel (including the trigger channel) begins in phase. This ensures the ADC trigger generated by the dedicated channel is aligned to the PWM period boundary from the first cycle.

At runtime, update the duty cycle for the three inverter phases. The function copies three requested duty values into the internal duty array—one per phase—and applies them immediately to all configured PWM channels in one operation. Complementary outputs inherit the new duty with the configured dead-time automatically. Expected input: requestDuty points to an array of three float32 values corresponding to phases A, B, and C, each normalized to the range 0.0 to 1.0 (representing 0% to 100% of the PWM period). No clamping or validation is performed: values outside [0.0, 1.0], NaN, or Inf are not checked and are forwarded to the driver as-is. The driver updates the hardware shadow registers and commits them immediately so the change takes effect without waiting for a sync event.

private_functions.behavior_description: Configure and route the eGTM ATOM output pins for the three complementary phase pairs (six PWM outputs) and the dedicated trigger channel. This function selects the appropriate ATOM TOUT pin mappings, sets port pad modes/drive strength as required, and performs any necessary DTM output routing for complementary signals. Parameter: 'reserved' is currently unused and should be set to 0. Call timing: invoked during initialization (from initEgtmAtom3phInv) after enabling the eGTM clock but before initializing the PWM channels so that pin routing is in place prior to enabling outputs.
